audience.title: Who telepace can talk to for you.

inbox.title: Everything that needs a human.
inbox.itemBody1: Interview 048 flagged distress signals — auto-closed. Review the transcript before dismissing.
inbox.itemBody2: New theme surfaced: '$79 feels punitive without SSO' (confidence 0.82, 11 supporting quotes).
inbox.itemBody3: Reached 24 / 24 completions. Study auto-closes tomorrow unless you extend it.
inbox.itemBody4: Weekly digest is ready — 3 studies progressed and 2 new themes surfaced.

insights.title: Themes, across every study.
insights.emptyDescription: Themes surface here once your studies start collecting responses.
insights.theme1Quote1: If SSO were included I wouldn't even blink at the price. Right now it feels like I'm paying to be inconvenienced.
insights.theme1Quote2: Every other tool at this price gives you Okta. It's table stakes.
insights.theme1Quote3: $79 with SSO = fine. $79 without = starting to look at competitors.
insights.theme2Title: Onboarding stops mid-flow after email confirmation
insights.theme2Quote1: I confirmed my email and… nothing. I forgot about it for two days.
insights.theme2Quote2: The email said 'you're in' but the app didn't seem to know that.
insights.theme3Title: The MCP hook is the reason for the upgrade
insights.theme3Quote1: The MCP integration is what made me pull out the card. Everything else is table stakes.
insights.theme3Quote2: My Claude does research now. That's magic. Ten times worth $79.

integrations.title: Where telepace connects.
integrations.emptyDescription: Connect Notion, Linear, Slack, or a webhook to send insights where your team already works.
integrations.mcpDetail: Let Claude, Cursor, or Codex run your research directly — create studies, query insights, and export evidence over MCP.

studies.title: What are we learning today?
studies.emptyDescription: Describe what you want to learn — the Designer agent drafts the interview for you.

studyDetail.studyLiveBannerBody: Send this link to the people you want to hear from. Interviews and insights land on this page as they complete.
studyDetail.copyFailDescription: Select the link and copy it manually.
studyDetail.publishSuccessDescription: Share the link to start collecting interviews.
studyDetail.closeSuccessDescription: The share link no longer accepts new interviews.
studyDetail.insightsAnalyzingBody: The Analyst agent is reading the transcript. Themes and verbatims land here in under a minute.
studyDetail.insightsWaitingLive: Share the link above — themes, verbatims and concerns appear here as interviews complete.
studyDetail.insightsWaitingDraft: Publish the study and share the link — insights appear as interviews complete.
studyDetail.noQuestionsYet: No questions yet — refine this study in the design chat.
studyDetail.closeDialogBody: The share link stops accepting new interviews. Completed interviews and insights stay available.

settings.title: Workspace preferences.
settings.mcpAuthHint: Authenticate with your API key above. Compatible with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.
settings.deleteWorkspaceWarning: All studies, transcripts, and insights will be permanently deleted after a 30-day grace period.

sectionDesc.workspace: How this workspace is named and addressed.
sectionDesc.members: Who can see and edit this workspace.
sectionDesc.mcp: Connect this workspace to your AI tools.

settings.workspaceNameHint: Shown across the app and in invitations.
settings.urlSlugHint: Used in URLs and your MCP endpoint.
settings.seatsHint: Roles control who can edit studies and view transcripts.
settings.keysHint: Keys are shown once at creation. Rotate immediately if exposed.
settings.deleteConfirmBody: This deletes all studies, transcripts, and insights after a 30-day grace period. To confirm, type the workspace slug {slug} below.

responses.title: Responses
responses.emptyDescription: Responses appear here as interviews complete.

charts.multiSelectTip: Percentages may exceed 100% — respondents could select multiple options.
charts.top2BoxNote: Top-2-Box: sum of the two most favorable responses.
charts.noData: Not enough data for charts yet.
charts.noDataDescription: Charts appear once three or more interviews have been analyzed.

followups.branchPlaceholder: e.g. Tell me more about that…
followups.welcomePlaceholder: Thank you for participating in this research…
followups.endPlaceholder: Thank you for your time! Your responses help us improve…

voice.title: Voice analysis
voice.noAudio: No audio available for this interview.
voice.clickToSeek: Click to jump to this segment

report.title: Report
report.noReportDescription: The report generates once enough interviews are analyzed.

newStudy.systemGreeting: Tell me what you'd like to learn. I'll draft the interview for you.
newStudy.suggestion1: Why did trial users churn before upgrading last quarter?
newStudy.suggestion2: How do freelancers decide which invoicing tool to pay for?
newStudy.suggestion3: First reactions to our new onboarding flow
newStudy.chatPlaceholder: Describe what you want to learn…
newStudy.outlinePlaceholder: Your outline appears here as we design it together.
newStudy.launchSubtitle: Choose how this study reaches people, then publish.
newStudy.publishThenDispatch: Then send to phone / email from the dispatch step.
newStudy.noTurns: No turns returned. Try another persona.
newStudy.clarifyLeadDecision: Got it. Before I draft — what decision should this research support? Pick any that fit, or tell me in your own words.
newStudy.clarifyLeadAudience: And who exactly are we listening to?
newStudy.audienceEndUsers: The people who use it daily
newStudy.seedDrafted: Drafted {parts}. Tell me what to sharpen.
newStudy.seedEmpty: I've drafted a starting outline. Anything to refine?
newStudy.draftedFallback: I've drafted a starting outline on the right. Anything to change?
newStudy.clarifyClosure: Your guide on the right is ready — simulate a respondent or publish whenever you like.
newStudy.guideUpdated: {count, plural, one {Guide updated: # change} other {Guide updated: # changes}}
newStudy.readinessCaptured: {label} captured — {remaining, plural, =0 {ready to publish} =1 {1 step still open} other {# steps still open}}
newStudy.readinessOpenHint: {remaining, plural, =1 {1 thing still open} other {# things still open}}
newStudy.gateReady: Got it — drafting your outline now…
newStudy.gateNotResearch: This doesn't quite read like a research need. Do you want to run a user study, or something else?
newStudy.taskRefineInstruction: Update the research task's "{facet}" to: {value}. Realign the outline, persona, and screener accordingly.
newStudy.canvasEmptyBody: Tell the designer what you want to learn on the left — the interview takes shape here.

wizard.goalTitle: What do you want to learn?
wizard.goalPlaceholder: e.g. Understand how users discover and evaluate new SaaS tools
wizard.audienceTitle: Who do you want to talk to?
wizard.audiencePlaceholder: e.g. Product managers at B2B SaaS companies
wizard.goalFieldPlaceholder: What should this question help you learn?

templates.title: Start from a template
templates.importDescription: Upload a discussion guide and we'll parse it into questions.

copilot.title: Research copilot
copilot.subtitle: Ask questions across all your studies.
copilot.noStudiesDescription: Create and run at least one study to use the copilot.
copilot.answer1: Across the studies you selected, pricing friction is the dominant theme. In {studies}, respondents repeatedly tie the $79 tier to a lack of SSO — the objection shows up in 11 of 34 completed interviews.
copilot.answer2: Two of the studies point the same direction: onboarding stalls after email confirmation. The pattern in {studies} suggests people never receive a clear next step, so activation drops within 48 hours.
copilot.answer3: Comparing {studies}, the MCP integration is the strongest expansion driver — it's the single feature respondents cite unprompted when explaining why they upgraded.
copilot.answer4: Synthesizing {studies}: churned users and active power-users describe the same gap. The difference is timing — power-users hit the wall later, which buys you a window to intervene.

clips.title: Highlight clips
clips.noClipsDescription: Audio clips are generated once voice interviews are analyzed.

mcp.title: MCP Research Tools
mcp.subtitle: Connect telepace to your AI coding assistant.
mcp.setupDescription: Add the telepace MCP server to your AI tool configuration:
mcp.toolCreateStudyDesc: Create a new research study with a goal and target audience.
mcp.toolQueryInsightsDesc: Query insights and themes from completed studies.
mcp.toolExportEvidenceDesc: Export evidence clips and citations from a study.
mcp.toolTriggerAnalysisDesc: Trigger the analysis pipeline for a study.
mcp.toolListStudiesDesc: List all studies in your workspace with status and metrics.

agent.title: Research Copilot
agent.greeting: I can create, track, analyze, and share studies. Try:
agent.suggestion1: Create a study about new-user onboarding
agent.suggestion2: List all my studies and their completion
agent.suggestion3: Analyze the key findings of my latest study
agent.placeholder: Ask the copilot to do something…
